context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements URL-driven filter state management: encoding multi-value filters as repeated query params, parsing on mount and popstate, using pushState for history, resetting page on filter change, and cleaning up empty filter keys.

checklist.name: Multi-value URL encoding
checklist.description: The URL builder appends multi-value filters using repeated query param keys (e.g., brand=Nike&brand=Adidas), not comma-joined or JSON-encoded values

checklist.name: URL parse function
checklist.description: A parse function reads URLSearchParams entries and groups values by key into arrays, skipping reserved keys (sort, page, q)

checklist.name: URL build function
checklist.description: A build function serializes a filters object back to a query string, including sort, page, and q parameters

checklist.name: Mount initialization
checklist.description: The hook initializes filter state by parsing the URL query string at mount time (not from a hardcoded default empty object)

checklist.name: Popstate listener
checklist.description: The hook adds a 'popstate' event listener to re-sync filter state when the browser navigates back or forward, and removes it on cleanup

checklist.name: pushState not replaceState
checklist.description: Filter changes call history.pushState() to add a new history entry, NOT history.replaceState()

checklist.name: Page reset on filter change
checklist.description: Toggling a filter resets the page to 1 (not preserving the current page number)

checklist.name: Empty key removal
checklist.description: When the last value is removed from a facet key, that key is deleted from the filters object entirely (not left as an empty array)

checklist.name: Clear all resets URL
checklist.description: The clearAll function resets the URL to '?' or removes all filter params, rather than leaving stale params in the URL

checklist.name: Notes document
checklist.description: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md explains multi-value URL representation, popstate handling, and empty-key removal behavior
